title: Seattle: Ella Bailey Park | Park Preview
content: ## A guide to Seattle area parks with an emphasis on accessibility, photography, and birds. # Seattle: Ella Bailey Park. ##### View of Seattle skyline and Mt. Rainier Scenic picnic area. Ella Bailey Park on Magnolia Hill is named for Ella Bailey, a Seattle school teacher for more than 30 years, who sold the land to the City of Seattle in 1929 with the provision that it be used as a children’s playfield. The property was originally leased by the city to the Seattle School District for use by the old Magnolia Elementary School, which closed in 1984 and was boarded up to all use in 2002. Ella Bailey Park is small, with a playground, central lawn, and picnic tables. Because of the playground, it makes a great Seattle viewpoint for families with children to visit. As long as you can park close to one of the entrances, the park is very accessible.
